leaves.title: Freeze the canonical specimen and naming discipline
leaves.objective: Lock the pilot to the canonical `opening_drive_continuation` / `continuation_ladder_exec` pair and record how the legacy `continuation_ladder` label survives as a compatibility bridge.
leaves.workflow_intent_assertion: This leaf sits at the naming and authority boundary before any new behaviour is allowed. It decides exactly which specimen the later lifecycle work is allowed to touch.
ordered_actions: Read the vocabulary authority and the checked-in registry surfaces end to end.
Freeze the canonical specimen and the legacy-bridge naming rules in the gate master and leaves ledger.
State explicitly which labels are canonical runtime truth and which survive only as compatibility bridges.
forbidden_actions: Do not let `continuation_ladder` remain the only canonical runtime label for this specimen.
Do not introduce new runtime names without first checking the vocabulary authority.
expected_evidence: planning docs name the canonical specimen explicitly
definition_of_done: the pack names the specimen and the live bridge surfaces explicitly
later leaves cannot broaden to other setup variants without amending the active pack first
leaves.packaging_requirement: If this leaf closes the gate, create a fresh full-history zip from the exact green repo state.

leaves.title: Trace the packet, carry, and ledger seams before writing behaviour

ordered_actions: Trace the ingress packet, egress packet, carry handoff, and symbol-level ledger surfaces through the live code path.
